title: Extraction of Uranium(VI) from Aqueous Nitrate Solution with Triisoamyl Phosphate
author: D. C. RUPAINWAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Applied Chemistry Section, School of Applied Sciences, Institute of Technology, Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi-221 005 

  Manuscript received 15 Julie 1982, revised 21 December 1982, accepted 30 March 1983  

 The higher homologue of  \(n\) -tributyl phosphate viz., triisoamyl phosphate (TAP) was prepared from fusel oil and has been   successfully employed for the extraction of uranium(VI) from its aqueous nitric acid solution and its separation from various cations and anions. Parameters like (TAP), [H 8 O + ], [U(VI)] and the effect of diluent employed have been investigated in detail. It was found that maximum extraction (95%) takes place at [H 8 O + ]=3.0  N  and by 20% TAP (v/v) in carbontetrachloride. The empirical formula of the extracted species has been evaluated to be UO 2 ,(NO 3 ) 2 .2TAP. The mechanism of the extraction process has been discussed.
